article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

reasoning: Multiple failures: flagged quoted/attributed source language as author bias, used non-standard/incorrect bias type labels, included questionable positives, and missed clear author-level spin in the opening setup.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged direct quotes from O’Rourke and an attributed statement from ISA’s chief as author bias, violating attribution rules.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Two of four items are purely quoted/attributed; the first is speculative selection critique; only the third could be a defensible author-level bias but was misframed.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-prose framing such as 'Once upon a time... Job done.' and 'Enter “Thinking Mode”.' and loaded terms like 'interrogate'/'put them on the spot.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used labels not in the allowed set (Selection bias, Framing bias, Authority bias) and misapplied 'Omission bias' instead of the defined 'Bias by Omission' or more fitting types like Unsubstantiated Claims/Opinion as Fact.
